description: A hinged or sliding barrier for closing an entrance; the movable boundary between spaces. 'Ponu' names what opens and closes—threshold that can yield or hold.
sound_symbolism: The word pivots passage. 'Po' swings with pivoting motion, door moving. Then 'nu' grounds the frame, passage defined.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo ponu' (the door). Works with 'kira' (key) for locked doors. Related to 'noshi' (threshold) as concept of transition.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Doors as choice—each door a decision point, what to enter, what to leave.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Threshold as boundary—the door separates inside from outside, private from public.
